title: Capacity Building for Regional Labs to Improve Salmonella Outbreak Response
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Salmonella     remains     one     of     the     leading     causes     of     foodborne     illnesses     worldwide  ,   imposing     a     significant     public     health     and     economic     burden  . Rapid   detection     and     response     to     Salmonella     outbreaks   are   essential     to   limit   morbidity  ,   mortality  ,   and     economic     losses  .   Strengthening     the     capacity     of   regional   laboratories     is     crucial     to     achieving     timely   diagnosis,   accurate     source     attribution  ,   and     effective     risk     communication  .   Building     laboratory     capacity     involves     improving     infrastructure  ,   training     skilled     personnel  ,   standardizing   testing   protocols  ,   and     ensuring   data   sharing     across     regions  .   Empowering   regional   labs     will     decentralize     outbreak     response  ,   reduce     turnaround     times  ,   and     enhance     collaboration     between     public     health     authorities     and     the     food     industry  .   This     approach     supports     national     food     safety     strategies     and     improves     consumer     trust   in   the     integrity     of     the     food     supply     chain  .
